name: Graubünden / Grisons / Grigioni / Grischun (Three Leagues)
summary: Graubünden (German: Graubünden / French: Grisons / Italian: Grigioni / Romansh: Grischun) is an Eastern Swiss civil-law constituent canton of the Swiss Confederation — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern jurisdiction formally operating Romansh language in formal-administrative usage (Romansh recognised as official cantonal language alongside German + Italian via Cantonal Constitution 2003 Article 3, with Romansh recognised as Swiss Federal national language under Federal Constitution 1996 Article 4 + Federal Languages Act 2007 — Graubünden is the only modern jurisdiction globally where Romansh operates as official cantonal language, distinct from rest-of-Swiss-Confederation where Romansh has Federal-but-not-cantonal status), as the only trilingual Swiss canton (German ~74% + Romansh ~14% + Italian ~12% — distinct from rest-of-Swiss-cantons which operate monolingual or bilingual frameworks), as the largest Swiss canton by area (~7,105 km² — ~17% of Swiss land area, larger than any other Swiss canton including Bern + Valais), as the only modern Swiss canton with continuous pre-Confederation Three Leagues (Drei Bünde / Tres Lias) framework predating Swiss Confederation incorporation (Three Leagues 1395-1803 — Lega Caddea Grey League 1395 + Lega da la Chadè God's House League 1367 + Lega da las Dieschdretgiras Ten Jurisdictions League 1436, formally pre-Swiss-Confederation independent confederation framework, only joined Swiss Confederation via Napoleonic Act of Mediation 19 February 1803 — distinct from rest-of-Swiss-Confederation framework where most cantons joined 1291-1513), and as the central jurisdiction of pre-Confederation Rhaeto-Romanic continuous historical-cultural-political-linguistic framework (Romansh language descended from Vulgar Latin Rhaeto-Romanic Roman framework continuous since 15 BCE Roman conquest of Raetia — only modern Western European language with continuous Vulgar Latin lineage outside Romance-language-mainstream). Graubünden was historically the central Roman Raetia + the Three Leagues 1395-1803 + Napoleonic Helvetic Republic 1798-1803 + Swiss Confederation incorporation 19 February 1803 framework. Graubünden comprises ~7,105 km² with a population of ~200,000. Family-law framework operates under the federal Swiss civil-law framework (Swiss Civil Code 1907 + Swiss Civil Procedure Code 2008 + Swiss Same-Sex Marriage Reform 2022) applied uniformly across Swiss cantons. The Cantonal Court of Graubünden is the apex regional court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Swiss Federal Supreme Court. Graubünden is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Switzerland is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory and party (acceded 11 October 1983 effective 1 January 1984) — Graubünden is governed by Swiss Hague-Convention-1980 framework.

statutory_framework.citation: Cantonal Constitution of Graubünden 2003 (Article 3 — Romansh as official language)
statutory_framework.title: Cantonal Constitution of Graubünden 2003
statutory_framework.relevance: Cantonal Constitution of Graubünden of 14 September 2003 effective 1 January 2004 — formal recognition of Romansh as official cantonal language alongside German + Italian.

statutory_framework.citation: Swiss Federal Constitution 1999 Article 4 (Romansh national language)
statutory_framework.title: Swiss Federal Constitution Article 4
statutory_framework.relevance: Swiss Federal Constitutional Article 4 recognising Romansh as national language of Switzerland — applicable to Graubünden.

statutory_framework.title: Swiss Federal Languages Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Swiss Federal Languages Act of 5 October 2007 — federal language framework applicable to Graubünden.

statutory_framework.title: Napoleonic Act of Mediation
statutory_framework.relevance: Napoleonic Act of Mediation of 19 February 1803 — formal incorporation of Three Leagues into Swiss Confederation as canton of Graubünden.

statutory_framework.citation: Swiss Civil Code 1907 + Swiss Same-Sex Marriage Reform 2022
statutory_framework.title: Swiss Civil Code + Same-Sex Marriage Reform
statutory_framework.relevance: Swiss Federal Civil Code 1907 Family Law portion applicable uniformly across Graubünden + 2022 Same-Sex Marriage Reform framework.

statutory_framework.title: Hague Convention 1980 — Switzerland
statutory_framework.relevance: Swiss Hague Convention 1980 accession 11 October 1983 effective 1 January 1984 — applicable to Graubünden.

apex_courts.name: Cantonal Court of Graubünden (Kantonsgericht Graubünden)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Graubünden.

apex_courts.name: Swiss Federal Supreme Court (Bundesgericht)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Graubünden.

professional_regulators.name: Swiss Federal Office of Public Health (Bundesamt für Gesundheit BAG)
professional_regulators.role: Swiss Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Graubünden.

anonymisation_convention: Graubünden family-court decisions are anonymised per Swiss court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Roman conquest of Raetia
key_developments.description: Roman conquest of Raetia 15 BCE — beginning of Vulgar Latin Rhaeto-Romanic linguistic framework continuous through modern Romansh.

key_developments.title: God's House League founded
key_developments.description: Lega da la Chadè God's House League founded 1367 — first of three Leagues constituting pre-Confederation Three Leagues framework.

key_developments.title: Grey League founded
key_developments.description: Lega Caddea Grey League founded 1395 — second of three Leagues, source of name 'Graubünden' (Grey League).

key_developments.title: Ten Jurisdictions League founded
key_developments.description: Lega da las Dieschdretgiras Ten Jurisdictions League founded 1436 — third of three Leagues.

key_developments.title: Three Leagues alliance formalised
key_developments.description: Three Leagues alliance formalised 1471 — formal pre-Swiss-Confederation independent confederation framework.

key_developments.title: Swiss Confederation incorporation
key_developments.description: Three Leagues formally incorporated into Swiss Confederation as canton of Graubünden via Napoleonic Act of Mediation 19 February 1803.

key_developments.title: Romansh recognised as Swiss national language
key_developments.description: Romansh recognised as Swiss Federal national language via Federal referendum 20 February 1938.

key_developments.title: Romansh recognised as Swiss official language
key_developments.description: Romansh recognised as Swiss Federal official language for communication with Romansh speakers via Federal referendum 10 March 1996.

key_developments.title: Cantonal Constitution of Graubünden
key_developments.description: Cantonal Constitution of Graubünden 14 September 2003 effective 1 January 2004 — formal recognition of Romansh as official cantonal language alongside German + Italian.

structural_findings: Graubünden operates a federal Swiss civil-law constituent canton framework — places Graubünden in the Eastern Swiss canton cluster.

Only modern jurisdiction formally operating Romansh language in formal-administrative usage is structurally distinctive globally — only modern jurisdiction where Romansh operates as official cantonal language.
Only trilingual Swiss canton (German + Romansh + Italian) is structurally distinctive within Swiss canton cluster — distinct from rest-of-Swiss-cantons monolingual or bilingual frameworks.
Largest Swiss canton by area (~7,105 km² / ~17% of Swiss land area) is structurally distinctive within Swiss canton cluster — larger than any other Swiss canton.

Only modern Swiss canton with continuous pre-Confederation Three Leagues (Drei Bünde / Tres Lias) framework predating Swiss Confederation incorporation is structurally distinctive globally — 1395-1803 Three Leagues framework + 1803 Napoleonic Act of Mediation incorporation.

Central jurisdiction of pre-Confederation Rhaeto-Romanic continuous historical-cultural-political-linguistic framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Western European language with continuous Vulgar Latin lineage outside Romance-language-mainstream.
Three-League historical federal-confederation framework (Grey League 1395 + God's House League 1367 + Ten Jurisdictions League 1436) is structurally distinctive globally.
editorial_notes: Graubünden jurisdiction sidecar — civil-law Eastern Swiss constituent canton (Cantonal Constitution of Graubünden 2003 Article 3 + Swiss Federal Constitution 1999 Article 4 + Swiss Federal Languages Act 2007 + Napoleonic Act of Mediation 1803 + Swiss Civil Code 1907 + Same-Sex Marriage Reform 2022 + Three Leagues 1395-1803 + Roman Raetia 15 BCE + Romansh national language 1938 + Romansh official language 1996 + Swiss Hague-1980). Only modern jurisdiction formally operating Romansh language in formal-administrative usage globally + only trilingual Swiss canton + largest Swiss canton by area + only modern Swiss canton with continuous pre-Confederation Three Leagues framework predating Swiss Confederation incorporation + central jurisdiction of pre-Confederation Rhaeto-Romanic continuous historical-cultural-political-linguistic framework + Vulgar Latin continuous linguistic lineage.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.
